commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0d07f1b753c03195d6dbdb148ae55f2bc4e58ffe)
commands.review_summary:
The PR captures an inherited `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_SERVICE_PID` before service-mode startup overwrites it, threads it as an extra protected PID through stale gateway cleanup, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +82, Tests +62. Total +144 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, not a live local repro: current main overwrites the inherited gateway PID before cleanup and cleanup only protects self plus ancestors, while Codex zsh snapshots can source `.zshrc`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is still needed for real after-fix host proof, and the patch needs a strict PID parsing fix before maintainer merge review can pass.

target.title: fix(infra): preserve inherited gateway PID across reparent during cleanup
target.body:
## Summary

Resubmit of #90802 (which was closed because the source branch carried unrelated WIP commits). This PR is now a single commit on top of `main` touching exactly 3 files.

`cleanStaleGatewayProcessesSync` already excludes the caller and its ancestor chain from kill candidates (#68451). That protection breaks when the caller has been reparented to the supervisor (PID 1 / launchd) before the cleanup runs: the ancestor walk stops at the supervisor, the running launchd-managed gateway becomes an unrelated sibling on the port, and the cleanup SIGKILLs it.

The fix captures `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_SERVICE_PID` from inherited env before the gateway-cli overwrites it with its own PID, then threads the captured value into the exclusion set so the launchd-managed parent is kept out of the kill list even across a reparent boundary.

The protection is opt-in per call site. `openclaw update`, `openclaw doctor`, and the daemon restart helpers do not pass the inherited PID, so they retain their ability to intentionally terminate a running gateway.

## Real-world reproduction

A user `~/.zshrc` containing this legacy pre-launchd auto-start block:

```bash
if ! pgrep -x "openclaw-gateway" > /dev/null; then
  (openclaw gateway >/dev/null 2>&1 &)
fi
```

combined with codex 0.135.0 spawning `/bin/zsh -c "set -e; . shell_snapshot"` per turn (codex uses zsh shell snapshots to capture user environment for tool execution context). Each turn:

1. Gateway A receives a chat request, spawns codex.
2. Codex spawns the per-turn shell snapshot. zsh sources `~/.zshrc`, hits the legacy block, the `pgrep -x openclaw-gateway` does not match A's process title in time, and the subshell starts a new `openclaw gateway` B in background.
3. The subshell exits; B is reparented to launchd.
4. B enters `runGatewayCommand`. `process.env[OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_SERVICE_PID]` still holds A's PID (inherited from gateway A through codex through zsh), but is immediately overwritten with B's own PID.
5. B calls `cleanStaleGatewayProcessesSync`. Ancestor walk sees `process.ppid === 1` (launchd) and stops there. A appears on lsof for the port as an unrelated PID. B SIGKILLs A.
6. launchd `KeepAlive: { Crashed: true }` respawns A. Cycle repeats on the next chat request.

A forensic launchd unified-log tracker on the affected host captured 33 such kill events between 2026-05-30 and 2026-06-05, each `gateway [PID] exited due to SIGKILL | sent by node[PID]` with the killer always being a new openclaw gateway instance whose env carried `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_SERVICE_PID` matching the victim. Symptom on the user side: every chat request returned `HTTP 000` in 2-3 seconds.

The host-side fix was deleting the legacy zshrc block. After deletion, gateway PID stayed stable across 15+ hours of continuous use with zero kill events. The openclaw cleanup primitive should not be reachable into a suicide-kill state via that path in the first place — that is what this PR addresses.

## Verification

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/restart-stale-pids.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  52 passed | 2 skipped (54)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  150 passed (150)

$ pnpm tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.json
(clean)
```

The two new tests in `src/infra/restart-stale-pids.test.ts`:

- `excludes the inherited gateway service PID when the caller has been reparented to launchd` stubs `process.ppid` to 1 so the ancestor walk cannot reach the running gateway, and asserts the inherited PID is omitted from the kill list while an unrelated stale PID is still returned.
- `ignores a non-positive inherited gateway PID rather than poisoning the kill list` documents the undefined/0/negative input contract so a future refactor cannot silently start excluding pid 0.

Existing #68451 ancestor-exclusion tests still pass and continue to cover the non-reparent path.

## Out of scope

- The legacy auto-start block in user `~/.zshrc` is host config; not addressed here.
- No `openclaw update` / `openclaw doctor` behavior changes. Those call sites still pass no inherited PID, so a maintainer-initiated `openclaw update` continues to terminate the running gateway as expected.
- Windows code path also accepts the new parameter but the suicide-kill scenario this fixes is shell-driven and Unix-only; the Windows path keeps API symmetry but the behavioral change is dormant there.
target.author: amittell
